title: We should take Eva less seriously
display_title: We should take Eva less seriously
wikitext:
[]: We should take Eva less seriously             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Thu Apr 22, 2010 10:55 pm J_Faulkner wrote: worry about what? Come on, out with it, no need to be shy. I'm all ears, and won't call you names or anything.

[]:             Quote Post by J_Faulkner » Thu Apr 22, 2010 11:02 pm SenorSquiid wrote: Sorry about that, but I kind of needed to get that off my chest.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Apr 23, 2010 7:44 am wat is this i dont even OMF, please oh please permit me this one omnislash. I promise I'll never ever do it again. This newfriend needs some education. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Fri Apr 23, 2010 1:48 pm Well I can't say you didn't warn me you were a self important pretentious blowhard.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Apr 23, 2010 4:08 pm If we have to go here, let's lay down some ground rules (i.e. remind you of the forum rules on posting)-- Circlejerking we can just about put up with. Flaming we won't.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Fri Apr 23, 2010 4:17 pm What the hell are you guys going on about? What was this split from?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Apr 23, 2010 4:34 pm SenorSquiid wrote: Rei's character in the show is pretty boring until the 2nd season kicks into gear. Misato can be kind of obnoxious until the 2nd season. Actually a lot of things about Eva are kind of uninteresting until the 2nd season kicks into gear. The first season has some quality parts like the Dance episode, filler sure, but really really well done entertaining filler, Eva is largely restricted from rising to classical levels of greatness by it's format, and by Gainax's intentions marketing wise.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Apr 23, 2010 5:00 pm BrikHaus wrote: Woah, woah, woah, woah, woah, woah, woah. Wait, wait, wait, wait, wait, wait, wait, wait, wait. Hold on second. You're saying.... there's a SECOND SEASON of Evangelion? Holy shit, how did I not know about this until right now?

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Apr 23, 2010 5:44 pm

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Apr 23, 2010 5:52 pm BrikHaus wrote: You're saying.... there's a SECOND SEASON of Evangelion? Holy shit, how did I not know about this until right now?

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Fri Apr 23, 2010 6:06 pm Oh man. Best post in months for sure. Thank you SenorSquiid on providing the information on what makes Evangelion is so obnoxious and irritating. Nearly everything you said I agree with at least to some extant. And yes, Asuka is an annoying, selfish bitch. It took me a while to figure that out as I matured, but she is completely full of herself and fans do anything they can to curve it the other way. And for the record Senor, this is a site called Evageeks , so naturally you're going to find rabid fanboys/fangirls that will indulge in everything that has to do with Evangelion, including UCC coffee cans, Eva bikes and character "pillows." God forbid telling them the show they worship is imperfect, but that's why I give you 5 stars on having the balls to write this in the first place. It's good to put everything into perspective once in a while, despite the shit that's bound to be thrown at something like this.

[]:             Quote Post by Peewit » Fri Apr 23, 2010 6:33 pm but his vision is compromised by the fact it was set up partly to sell merch, and appeal to otakus.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Apr 23, 2010 6:42 pm Actually, in my opinion Evangelion didn't start to get really great until halfway through the third season. I mean, the second season was good , but in the third season we actually get answers like who shot Kaji and how PenPen developed his cocaine addiction. I for one felt that the amazing CGI pyrotechnics sequence in episode 62 completely made up for the reused footage in previous episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 23, 2010 6:43 pm Ironfoot wrote: Oh man. Best post in months for sure.

[]:             Quote Post by TheSpleenDilemma » Fri Apr 23, 2010 6:50 pm Let's face it people, Evangelion is supposed to be entertaining. It's a Anime television show, first and foremost. Isn't Anno's vision meant to be moving, but in an enjoyable way? That's how it really should be. I'm not doubting it's depth of character or the phycological aspects, but they should just add to the viewing. For some, fan-wanking is part of the full enjoyment of the show. Let them have their fun. Is it really harming anybody? I know this is a bit contradictory, but comparing Shakespere to Evangelion? No man, just no.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Apr 23, 2010 7:01 pm TheSpleenDilemma wrote: I know this is a bit contradictory, but comparing Shakespere to Evangelion? No man, just no.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Apr 23, 2010 7:09 pm TheSpleenDilemma wrote: I know this is a bit contradictory, but comparing Shakespere to Evangelion? No man, just no.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 23, 2010 7:12 pm TheSpleenDilemma wrote: For some, fan-wanking is part of the full enjoyment of the show. Let them have their fun. Is it really harming anybody?

[]:             Quote Post by TheSpleenDilemma » Fri Apr 23, 2010 7:17 pm I misread your post, I thought you were comparing them on the basis of quality. You were comparing them to his critiques, I understand that now. Comparing Shakespere's works and Evangelion is an unfair comparison, as they both are in a completely different format, with different pacing and style. It would be too hard to compare in a general sence, because you would inevitably be comparing the narrative structure of Asian and Western storytelling. I'm saying that it since they are told so differently, it would be very hard to compare Evangelion and only Evangelion to some of Shakespere's work. It would be much more interesting to compare a classic or definitive Japanese work, with that of Shakespere. Both could represent Eastern and Western narrative structures. Edit: Or I'm just a random 14 year old spewing crap out. My god, I had no idea fanwanking was so terrible! I thought it just lead to a lack of physical stimulus and romantic interactions of the alleged Fan-Wanker.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Apr 23, 2010 7:32 pm NAveryW wrote: Actually, in my opinion Evangelion didn't start to get really great until halfway through the third season. I mean, the second season was good , but in the third season we actually get answers like who shot Kaji and how PenPen developed his cocaine addiction. I for one felt that the amazing CGI pyrotechnics sequence in episode 62 completely made up for the reused footage in previous episodes.
